paper_title: Pollen and Aeroallergen Sampling in Córdoba

methods.description: Continuous pollen sampling was conducted throughout 2011 and 2012 using a Hirst-type 7-day recording volumetric trap to capture and analyze pollen grains in the air.

steps.description: Pollen sampling was performed continuously throughout 2011 and 2012, using a Hirst-type 7-day recording volumetric trap with a suction flow rate of 10 L/min.
steps.searchable_summary: set up and operate a Hirst-type volumetric trap for continuous pollen sampling

steps.description: Silicone fluid was used as adhesive for pollen capture.
steps.searchable_summary: apply silicone fluid as adhesive for pollen capture

steps.description: Samples were prepared and analysed counting four longitudinal transects along the slide, following the procedure recommended by the Spanish Aerobiology Network (REA).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze pollen samples by counting four longitudinal transects

steps.description: Data were expressed as daily average of pollen grains per cubic metre of air.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate daily average of pollen grains per cubic metre

methods.description: Protocol to define the start and end of the pollen season based on daily pollen counts.

steps.description: The start of the pollen season was defined as the first day on which a daily average of at least 1 pollen grains/m³ was detected, followed by five subsequent days with 1 or more pollen grains/m³.
steps.searchable_summary: determine start of pollen season based on daily pollen counts

steps.description: The end of the pollen season was defined as the last day on which a daily average of at least 1 pollen grains/m³ was recorded when counts for the five following days were below this level.
steps.searchable_summary: determine end of pollen season based on daily pollen counts

steps.description: The peak date was defined as the day on which maximum pollen counts were recorded.
steps.searchable_summary: identify peak date of pollen season

steps.description: The pollen index (PI) was defined as the sum of daily values during the pollen season.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate pollen index as sum of daily values

methods.description: Quantification of aeroallergens using a continuous wind-oriented multi-vial cyclone sampler during the Platanus pollen season.

steps.description: A continuous wind-oriented multi-vial cyclone sampler was used for the aeroallergen quantification with a suction flow rate of 16.5 L/min.
steps.searchable_summary: operate a multi-vial cyclone sampler for aeroallergen quantification

steps.description: Daily airborne particles were collected dry, directly into a 1.5 mL Eppendorf vial, from February to May.
steps.searchable_summary: collect daily airborne particles into Eppendorf vials

steps.description: Daily airborne samples were stored at -20 °C.
